title: Luxury Product Showcase Configurator
description: An interactive luxury product display where users arrange an Avocado and a Rolex watch on a rotating showcase pedestal, configuring their positions, scales, materials, and lighting to compose the perfect promotional shot. A budget system conserves total spotlight energy across three configurable lights, and full undo/redo tracks every change.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load Avocado.glb and rolex.glb via GLTFLoader and place them on a circular glossy pedestal (MeshStandardMaterial, metalness 0.3, roughness 0.1). The Rolex sits at center, the Avocado to its right. Implement OrbitControls for camera orbit. Camera starts at position (0, 1.5, 3) looking at origin. Use an HDR-style environment with PMREMGenerator from a simple procedural sky or neutral equirect. Add three SpotLights (key, fill, rim) with colored cones visible via SpotLightHelper. Implement a left-side HTML panel with these interactions: (1) Click-select either product to highlight it with an outline or emissive pulse. (2) Drag-translate the selected object on the pedestal XZ plane. (3) Scale slider per object (0.5x-2.0x). (4) Rotation Y slider per object (0-360 degrees). (5) A material toggle per object switching between original PBR and a gold-chrome override (metalness 1.0, roughness 0.05, color gold). (6) Three light intensity sliders (0-100 each) bound by a conservation law: total energy is fixed at 150, so increasing one auto-decreases others proportionally. (7) Light color pickers for each of the three spots. (8) A pedestal rotation toggle (auto-spin on/off at 0.3 rad/s). (9) Background color picker for the scene. (10) Undo and Redo buttons tracking all state changes (position, scale, rotation, material, lights, background) using a history stack of snapshots (max 50). Display a small status bar showing step count and current energy distribution. Ensure smooth 60fps rendering, proper shadow mapping (2048 shadow map on key light), and antialiased renderer. The panel should be semi-transparent dark glass style overlaying the left edge.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  avocadoPositionX: number,         // X position of Avocado on pedestal
  avocadoPositionZ: number,         // Z position of Avocado on pedestal
  avocadoScale: number,         // Scale multiplier for Avocado (0.5-2.0)
  avocadoRotationY: number,         // Y-axis rotation of Avocado in radians
  avocadoMaterialOverride: boolean,         // true if gold-chrome material applied to Avocado
  rolexScale: number,         // Scale multiplier for Rolex (0.5-2.0)
  rolexRotationY: number,         // Y-axis rotation of Rolex in radians
  rolexMaterialOverride: boolean,         // true if gold-chrome material applied to Rolex
  keyLightIntensity: number,         // Intensity of key spotlight (0-100)
  fillLightIntensity: number,         // Intensity of fill spotlight (0-100)
  rimLightIntensity: number,         // Intensity of rim spotlight (0-100)
  totalLightEnergy: number,         // Sum of all three light intensities (should equal 150)
}
